title: Github Low Priority Planning Lanes P3

execplan_profile.projection_rule: canonical_core is authoritative for intent, scope, next action, proof, continuation, and closeout; legacy fields remain compatibility projections.

canonical_core.requested_outcome: Issue intake found these are low-priority parent lanes, pilots, downstream SkillSpec work, or previously deferred friction lanes; when promoted, they should harden context-reduction behavior through real dogfooding rather than expanding visible methodology.
canonical_core.hard_constraints: Keep scope bounded to the promoted TODO item and its stated touched paths.
canonical_core.agent_may_decide: Bounded decomposition, touched-path narrowing, validation tightening, and plan-local residue routing.
canonical_core.escalate_when: A better-looking fix changes the requested outcome, owned surface, time horizon, or meaningful validation story.
canonical_core.next_action: For dogfooding, use #595 to measure context burden and artifact footprint on one real host-repo slice; for SkillSpec work, start with #961 and encode proportionality/no-artifact-by-default semantics before generated skill follow-ons.
completion_criteria: Lower-priority planning lanes remain visible while future SkillSpec and dogfooding work proves proportionality: compact guidance should prevent over-producing reviews, Memory notes, execplans, or raw context for routine tasks.
canonical_core.continuation_owner: GitHub #595 for real host-repo dogfooding; GitHub #962/#989 remain deferred codegen/plugin follow-ons.
